params.name: Raman Spectroscopy
params.description:
On Jul 26, 1928 AD, the Indian physicist Raman made a new discovery in his experiments.
In experiments, Raman discovered that light passing through a transparent medium would be scattered by molecules, causing a change in the frequency of the light. Raman found that the frequency of the scattered light is symmetrically distributed on both sides of the incident light frequency in the scattering spectrum. This discovery revealed the energy change that occurs when photons refract and has great v …
